Balanced resuscitation in trauma management involves maintaining proper levels of various physiological indicators while avoiding excessive fluid load. Recent literature emphasizes the importance of using smaller boluses and focusing on the quality of resuscitation via balanced blood product usage instead of relying heavily on crystalloids alone. Therefore, option D aligns best with modern approaches to trauma resuscitation—maintaining airway, breathing, and circulation concurrently—is supported directly by the articles discussing how each component needs attention alongside others for overall effective resuscitation.
